title: Unique Paths
problem_statement: Count the number of unique paths from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of an m x n grid, moving only down or right.
intuition: Every cell can be reached from above or from the left, so add the path counts from those cells.

approaches.name: Grid DP
approaches.description: Compute dp[i][j] as the sum of dp[i-1][j] and dp[i][j-1], with boundaries initialized to 1.
approaches.why_not: Computing all paths recursively is exponential without reuse.

interview_qa.a: The first row and first column have exactly one path each.

interview_qa.q: Why can space be reduced to one row?
interview_qa.a: Current row values only depend on the current row left cell and the previous row same column.
